note: Galvanostatic at locked I_amp=0.6 e/fs, one decade lower than the integration test's range. Tests whether the lock-in fits stay clean as we approach the experimentally-relevant frequencies (sim f_W maps to real-cell f via docs/eis_similitude.md).
Same amplitude, decade lower than doe-001. ~1.5 hours estimated. Will tell us whether the |Z| ~ 1/f trajectory continues into the spec ultra-LF region or transitions to a Randles arc.

10x lower amplitude than doe-001, same freq band. Tests the hypothesis that doe-001's R² drop at LF is driven by V_amp leaving linear regime. If doe-003 R²(V) stays > 0.95 across all 4 freqs, hypothesis confirmed and we extend at I=0.06 for the LF arc.
HF extension of integration test range. Looks for the Randles upper asymptote (R_s) where -Im(Z) → 0 and Re(Z) approaches the series-resistance limit. Fast (~3 min).

3x higher amplitude than doe-001 in same freq band. Counterpart to doe-003. We expect this to show even worse R²(V) at LF if the saturation hypothesis is right (reproducing the cycle-3b finding that I=2 gives lower R² than I=0.6 at all freqs).

LF extension at low amplitude — only useful if doe-003 confirms the linearity hypothesis. South can run this opportunistically; if doe-003 fails the hypothesis, we'll queue a STOP and pivot before doe-006 contributes meaningful data.
HF retry at I=5 (doe-004 used I=0.6 and starved below noise at f≥1e-2). Predicted V_amp at f=5e-2: 5×0.0013×(5/0.6)≈54 mV. Should land in linear regime with R² > 0.95.
Mid-amplitude HF — bridges doe-004 (I=0.6) and doe-007 (I=5). Three-point amplitude scan at the same freq band.
Far-HF at high amplitude. Avoids approaching sim-Nyquist (dt=5 fs ⇒ Nyquist=0.1/fs); f_max=5e-2 stays comfortably below.

LF at proportionally lower amplitude (10× smaller than doe-006). Tests whether the sliding-amplitude rule holds at LF — if we keep V_amp roughly constant by scaling I with f, R² should stay good across the full spec range.

Spec ultra-LF at sliding amplitude. Long job (~16 hr at 80 steps/sec) — south can run this in background while higher-priority jobs fan out. If R² stays high at appropriate amplitude, this validates the spec range that cycle 1's overnight run failed at.

Replication of doe-001 with different seed to measure stochastic spread of |Z|, phase, R²(V). With deterministic step ordering this should be ~1% spread; non-deterministic threading may give more. Important for setting confidence intervals.

Bridge band — between integration test (5e-4 to 5e-3 covered) and doe-001 / doe-003. I=0.6 here was Cycle 3d's locked config; replicating with the binary gives a fresh data point and bridges the freq gap.
Bridge band at the clean amplitude (I=0.06 from doe-003). Extends the clean-Nyquist window from [5e-5, 5e-4] up to [5e-5, 5e-3] if R² stays > 0.95.
Bridge band, very low amplitude. Probably below noise floor at HF end of band but tests the lower bound.
Even lower amplitude at LF — fourth amplitude at the same band as doe-002/006/010, completing a 4-amp scan. Will tell us how small I can go before V drops below noise.
Replication of doe-003 with different seed. doe-012 showed >3% spread across seeds in clean regime. Confirming for the I=0.06 case to ensure our 'gold standard' is reproducible.

Same as doe-014 but with 2x recording periods (8 instead of 4). Earlier cycle 3c/3e showed more periods don't help with quantization-driven residuals; checking whether that holds at low amplitude where the V signal is cleaner.

High-resolution version of doe-003 — covers 1.3 decades at 4 points/decade for ~6 points, vs doe-003's 4 points/decade at 1 decade. Will give a smoother Nyquist arc in the cleanest band, useful for fitting C and any visible R_ct.

Ultra-LF at proper sliding amplitude. doe-011 (I=0.0006) is predicted to saturate (V_amp~6V at f=5e-7); I=6e-5 should give V_amp~60 mV (linear). 16 hour wall — runs alongside doe-011 so we get both saturation envelope and clean linear data at ultra-LF.

Middle amplitude in [5e-6, 5e-5] band. Past the arc apex, |Z| should plateau toward R_s + R_ct (estimated ~30 from doe-019 R_ct tail). At I=0.002, V_amp = 0.002 × 30 ≈ 60 mV — should land in linear regime if extrapolation is right. Bridges between doe-010 (I=0.006, possibly saturating) and doe-016 (I=0.0006, possibly noise-floor-limited).

Ultra-LF at smaller amp than doe-020. With doe-020 (I=6e-5) and doe-011 (I=0.0006) bracketing, this fills below. Predicted V_amp at f=5e-7 (extrapolating |Z|~1380 from 1/f trend): 28 mV — clean if cell still in arc-ascent regime.
Ultra-LF middle amplitude — between doe-011 (I=0.0006) and doe-020 (I=6e-5). Predicted V_amp at f=5e-7: 280 mV (might saturate) — useful to bracket where the linearity ceiling falls at ultra-LF.

High-resolution sweep between doe-010's two clean points (f=5e-5 and f=1.58e-5). 0.5-decade band at 10 points/decade gives 6 points spanning the Warburg-shoulder region where Re(Z) jumps from +0.74 to +5.50. Should resolve the shape of Re(Z)/-Im(Z) growth — phase trajectory and slope diagnostics for capacitor-to-Warburg crossover.

Amplitude refinement at half doe-016's I. Predicted V_amp at f=5e-6 = 0.0003 × 138 ≈ 41 mV — well in linear regime, smaller than doe-016's V=83 mV. If Re(Z) at f=5e-6 here matches doe-016's +16.2, we have linear-regime confirmation. If it differs significantly, doe-016 was itself nonlinearity-inflated.

Replication of doe-016 with different seed — stochastic spread measurement at the LF arc. Critical given we plan to use doe-016's Re=+16.2 as the reference linear-regime value at f=5e-6 in the final Phase 1.3 plot.

Amplitude scan around I=0.06 with focused frequency window — see how Re(Z) climb from 0 to +0.7 across [5e-5, 2e-4] varies with I. Lowest of the new bracket; predicted V_amp at f=5e-5 = 0.02 × 15 = 300 mV (mid-saturation regime, but I=0.06 was 947 mV at same freq with R²=0.998, so should produce clean fit).
Mid-low of the new amplitude bracket. V_amp at f=5e-5 ≈ 0.04 × 15 = 600 mV.
Mid-high of bracket. V_amp at f=5e-5 ≈ 0.08 × 15 = 1.2 V (heavily past linear, but I=0.06 already there). Tests whether the R²~0.998 cleanliness extends or breaks.
Highest of the new bracket. V_amp at f=5e-5 ≈ 0.10 × 15 = 1.5 V. Together with doe-027/028/029 gives 5-amplitude scan (incl. doe-019 I=0.06) at consistent freq resolution in the cleanest band.
linear baseline mid (cos waveform)
linear baseline LF (cos waveform)
linear baseline HF (cos waveform)
mid-NL mid — direct comparison vs doe-003 sin (cos)
mid-NL LF — does +Im anomaly disappear under cos?
mid-NL HF — does small -Re disappear under cos?
intermediate amp scan (cos waveform)
saturation regime — does breakdown un-flip under cos?
LF apex search (small amp avoids saturation; 1/f extrapolation predicts V_amp ~130 mV at f=5e-6)
linear plateau confirmation, mid band
linear plateau confirmation, LF band
amp=0.04 HF gap-fill
amp=0.04 LF gap-fill
amp=0.10 HF gap-fill
deep breakdown sweep (V_amp may exceed sat ceiling)
extreme breakdown sweep
ultra-low amp — noise-floor characterization
dense sweep ppd=10 (was ppd=4 at narrower range)
new intermediate amp — fills 0.04→0.06 gap
dense sweep ppd=10 (was ppd=3)
new intermediate amp — fills 0.06→0.08 gap
new intermediate amp — fills 0.06→0.10 gap
dense sweep ppd=10 (was ppd=4 at narrower range)
Randles apex hunt — A=0.0003 keeps V_amp below 110 mV ceiling across the band; ~3 points to bracket the apex below f=5e-6.
Low-current fill between doe-cos-017/024 (A=3e-4) and the apex-pinning A=1e-4 sweep. V_amp predicted ~22-110 mV across the band — clean linear regime.
Apex overlap with doe-cos-024 at half the current — checks linear-regime invariance of the apex location.

Apex pinning sweep — extends LF reach below doe-cos-024. A=1e-4 keeps V_amp ≤ ~150 mV at f=1e-6 (linear ceiling). Long: ~20h. Brackets apex between bracketing doe-cos-024 (which placed it at f≈2-5e-7).
DCR Phase 1: low-amplitude baseline (analog of ~C/40 for our cell). 5 pulses, 2 fs sample stride. Test 2-RC fit quality at small-signal regime.
DCR Phase 1: mid-low amplitude.
DCR Phase 1: linear-regime baseline (matches our EIS amp=0.02 reference).
DCR Phase 1: mild-NL regime.
DCR Phase 1: NL envelope.
DCR Phase 1: saturation-envelope regime.
DCR Phase 1: deep breakdown — analog of 2C-3C in real cell. Tests 2-RC fit robustness.

DCR Phase 1: ultra-low amplitude with longer pulse window. Tests whether the slow arc tau2 saturates within an extended pulse — addresses the smoke-test finding that tau2 was extrapolating to ~200,000 fs at 2000-fs pulse durations.
